symptoms.symptom_name: Well-demarcated erythema over both cheeks and nasal bridge, strictly sparing the nasolabial folds (malar/butterfly distribution)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The malar rash sparing the nasolabial folds is the classic sign of acute cutaneous lupus.
Rosacea typically involves the nasolabial folds and central face with telangiectasia/papulopustules.
Cellulitis is usually unilateral and does not respect the butterfly distribution.

symptoms.symptom_name: Burning facial erythema triggered by sun exposure (photosensitivity)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Photosensitivity with a rapid post-UV facial eruption is characteristic of acute cutaneous lupus.
Also photosensitive, but usually itchy papules/plaques on intermittently sun-exposed sites rather than a malar burning erythema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid onset within ~4 hours of afternoon sun exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear UV trigger with rapid onset fits a photosensitive cutaneous flare.
A pre-existing positive ANA is the serological substrate for a photosensitive cutaneous lupus flare.
Erysipelas/cellulitis of the face is typically febrile with systemic toxicity.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Systemic lupus flare with organ involvement (e.g. nephritis, serositis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever is reassuring but does not by itself exclude a systemic flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dyspnoea, no pleuritic chest pain, no joint swelling, normal urine output and colour

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Systemic lupus flare with organ involvement (e.g. nephritis, serositis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative serositis, arthritis and renal screen argues strongly against concurrent systemic organ involvement at this time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin warm but not indurated/fluctuant; no pustules, weeping or crusting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of induration/fluctuance and purulence makes an infective cellulitis less likely — but this requires palpation the AI cannot do.
Contact dermatitis is usually eczematous, vesicular and weeping rather than a dry burning erythema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiovascular and respiratory examination, soft abdomen, no peripheral oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Systemic lupus flare with organ involvement (e.g. nephritis, serositis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician examination corroborating no serositis or fluid overload — sealed clinician measurement the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Home vital signs within normal limits (BP 125/80, HR 88, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Systemic lupus flare with organ involvement (e.g. nephritis, serositis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal haemodynamics argue against a systemic crisis; these were clinician-measured and, without a home device, are not obtainable in chat — the AI infers wellbeing from the patient's report, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Confirmatory serology and urinalysis (anti-dsDNA, complement C3/C4, FBC, urine protein/casts)

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected reassuring given the negative systemic screen; formally excludes lupus nephritis/serositis and gauges disease activity.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A subclinical systemic flare could be missed if never arranged.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Systemic lupus flare with organ involvement (e.g. nephritis, serositis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Urinalysis and complement/anti-dsDNA are how systemic involvement is formally excluded — hospital/GP investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Photosensitive malar eruption in an ANA-positive host
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these form the classic acute cutaneous lupus picture and drive the leading diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Localised cutaneous flare without systemic involvement
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The negative screen is what de-escalates an alarming presentation to a low-acuity one and excludes cellulitis and systemic flare.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urinalysis (dipstick for protein and blood; microscopy for casts)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Simple, key screen to exclude lupus nephritis — the organ involvement the patient fears; can be arranged at the in-person review.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods: FBC, anti-dsDNA, complement C3/C4, ESR/CRP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses lupus disease activity and helps exclude a systemic flare; also CRP helps weigh infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person dermatological examination of the face
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Palpation and close inspection to confirm the malar pattern and exclude facial cellulitis — the exam telehealth cannot perform.
Not indicated for a non-purulent, non-broken photosensitive erythema; would be over-investigation.
